My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

business_data = locals().get('var_functions.query_db_26', [])
business_df = pd.DataFrame(business_data) if business_data else pd.DataFrame()

print('Business dataframe shape:', business_df.shape)
print('Business columns:', business_df.columns.tolist() if not business_df.empty else 'No data')
print()

if not business_df.empty:
    print('Business data:')
    print(business_df[['business_id', 'name', 'review_count']].to_string())
    print()

    # Extract state from description
    state_map = {
        'CA': 'California', 'MO': 'Missouri', 'ID': 'Idaho', 
        'FL': 'Florida', 'IN': 'Indiana'
    }
    
    states = []
    for desc in business_df['description']:
        found_state = 'Unknown'
        for code, name in state_map.items():
            if f', {code},' in desc or f', {code}' in desc:
                found_state = name
                break
        states.append(found_state)
    
    business_df['state'] = states
    print('States extracted:')
    print(business_df[['name', 'state', 'review_count']].to_string())
    print()
    
    # Count reviews by state
    state_counts = business_df.groupby('state')['review_count'].apply(lambda x: pd.to_numeric(x).sum()).reset_index()

    print(state_counts.to_string())
    print()
    
    # Get state with highest review count
    state_counts['review_count'] = pd.to_numeric(state_counts['review_count'])
    top_state = state_counts.loc[state_counts['review_count'].idxmax()]

    result = {
        'business_count': len(business_df),
        'states_found': business_df['state'].unique().tolist(),
        'top_state': top_state['state'],
        'top_state_reviews': int(top_state['review_count']),
        'state_breakdown': state_counts.to_dict('records')
    }
else:

var_functions.query_db:10.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

import json
import pandas as pd
import re

# Get business data
print('Starting business data processing...')

# Access the stored business data
business_data = var_functions.query_db_36
print(f'Business data type: {type(business_data)}')
print(f'Business data length: {len(business_data)}')

# Create DataFrame
business_df = pd.DataFrame(business_data)
print(f'Business DataFrame shape: {business_df.shape}')
print(f'Columns: {business_df.columns.tolist()}')

# Extract state from description
# Look for 2-letter state codes in the description
state_patterns = {
    'CA': 'California',
    'MO': 'Missouri', 
    'ID': 'Idaho',
    'FL': 'Florida',
    'IN': 'Indiana'
}

def extract_state(description):
    if not description or not isinstance(description, str):
        return 'Unknown'
    
    # Look for pattern: text, ST (2-letter code)
    match = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\b', description)
    if match:
        state_code = match.group(1)
        return state_patterns.get(state_code, 'Unknown')
    return 'Unknown'

business_df['state'] = business_df['description'].apply(extract_state)

# Convert review_count to numeric
business_df['review_count'] = pd.to_numeric(business_df['review_count'], errors='coerce')

# Sum reviews by state
state_reviews = business_df.groupby('state')['review_count'].sum().reset_index()
state_reviews = state_reviews.sort_values('review_count', ascending=False)

print('\nReview counts by state:')
print(state_reviews.to_string(index=False))

# Get top state
top_state_row = state_reviews.iloc[0]
top_state = top_state_row['state']
top_state_review_count = int(top_state_row['review_count'])

print(f'\nTop state: {top_state} with {top_state_review_count} reviews')

result = {
    'top_state': top_state,
    'total_reviews': top_state_review_count,
    'state_breakdown': state_reviews.to_dict('records')
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:10.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

import json
import pandas as pd
import re

# Get business data
print('Starting business data processing...')

# Access the stored business data
business_data = var_functions.query_db_36
print(f'Business data type: {type(business_data)}')
print(f'Business data length: {len(business_data)}')

# Create DataFrame
business_df = pd.DataFrame(business_data)
print(f'Business DataFrame shape: {business_df.shape}')
print(f'Columns: {business_df.columns.tolist()}')

# Extract state from description
# Look for 2-letter state codes in the description
state_patterns = {
    'CA': 'California',
    'MO': 'Missouri', 
    'ID': 'Idaho',
    'FL': 'Florida',
    'IN': 'Indiana'
}

def extract_state(description):
    if not description or not isinstance(description, str):
        return 'Unknown'
    
    # Look for pattern: text, ST (2-letter code)
    match = re.search(r',\s*([A-Z]{2})\b', description)
    if match:
        state_code = match.group(1)
        return state_patterns.get(state_code, 'Unknown')
    return 'Unknown'

business_df['state'] = business_df['description'].apply(extract_state)

# Convert review_count to numeric
business_df['review_count'] = pd.to_numeric(business_df['review_count'], errors='coerce')

# Sum reviews by state
state_reviews = business_df.groupby('state')['review_count'].sum().reset_index()
state_reviews = state_reviews.sort_values('review_count', ascending=False)

print('\nReview counts by state:')
print(state_reviews.to_string(index=False))

# Get top state
top_state_row = state_reviews.iloc[0]
top_state = top_state_row['state']
top_state_review_count = int(top_state_row['review_count'])

print(f'\nTop state: {top_state} with {top_state_review_count} reviews')

result = {
    'top_state': top_state,
    'total_reviews': top_state_review_count,
    'state_breakdown': state_reviews.to_dict('records')
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

import json
import pandas as pd
import re

# Get business data
business_data = var_functions.query_db_36

# Create DataFrame
business_df = pd.DataFrame(business_data)

# Extract state from description
state_map = {'CA': 'California', 'MO': 'Missouri', 'ID': 'Idaho', 'FL': 'Florida', 'IN': 'Indiana'}

def extract_state(description):
    if not description or not isinstance(description, str):
        return 'Unknown'
    for code, state_name in state_map.items():
        if f', {code},' in description or f', {code}' in description:
            return state_name
    return 'Unknown'

business_df['state'] = business_df['description'].apply(extract_state)

# Convert review_count to numeric
business_df['review_count'] = pd.to_numeric(business_df['review_count'], errors='coerce')

# Sum reviews by state
state_reviews = business_df.groupby('state')['review_count'].sum().reset_index()

# Remove Unknown and sort
state_reviews = state_reviews[state_reviews['state'] != 'Unknown'].sort_values('review_count', ascending=False)

# Get top state
top_state = state_reviews.iloc[0]['state']
top_reviews = int(state_reviews.iloc[0]['review_count'])

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'top_state': top_state,
    'total_reviews': top_reviews,
    'state_breakdown': state_reviews.to_dict('records')
}))

import json
import pandas as pd
import re

# Get business data
business_data = var_functions.query_db_36

# Create DataFrame
business_df = pd.DataFrame(business_data)

# Extract state from description
state_map = {'CA': 'California', 'MO': 'Missouri', 'ID': 'Idaho', 'FL': 'Florida', 'IN': 'Indiana'}

def extract_state(description):
    if not description or not isinstance(description, str):
        return 'Unknown'
    for code, state_name in state_map.items():
        if f', {code},' in description or f', {code}' in description:
            return state_name
    return 'Unknown'

business_df['state'] = business_df['description'].apply(extract_state)

# Convert review_count to numeric
business_df['review_count'] = pd.to_numeric(business_df['review_count'], errors='coerce')

# Sum reviews by state
state_reviews = business_df.groupby('state')['review_count'].sum().reset_index()

# Remove Unknown and sort
state_reviews = state_reviews[state_reviews['state'] != 'Unknown'].sort_values('review_count', ascending=False)

# Get top state
top_state = state_reviews.iloc[0]['state']
top_reviews = int(state_reviews.iloc[0]['review_count'])

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'top_state': top_state,
    'total_reviews': top_reviews,
    'state_breakdown': state_reviews.to_dict('records')
}))

var_functions.query_db:10.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

var_functions.query_db:10.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!
